content:
- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md after loading prior progress.
- Found many tasks with <passes>false</passes>, so selection had to come from unfinished/broken tasks (meta-task rule not applicable yet).
- Chose .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/08-task-ha-worker-select-loop-and-action-dispatch.md as next task because pending ultra_high chain (12 -> 13 -> 15) is currently blocked by 08; doing 08 is the highest-priority unblocked move that respects priority and story-chain constraints.

- Read prior progress log, loaded `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and confirmed active task is `08-task-ha-worker-select-loop-and-action-dispatch.md`.
- Per workflow, stayed in planning-only mode. Gathered implementation context in parallel across HA worker/state/actions/decide, DCS store/keys, process worker contracts, watch channel APIs, worker contract tests, and Make gates.
- Found HA worker is still stubbed (`run` sleep loop + no-op `step_once`) and `HaWorkerCtx` still placeholder; confirmed this task truly starts from wiring phase.
- Wrote a full detailed `<execution_plan>` into task file with explicit file-level steps, dispatch semantics, trait changes, tests, and full gate sequence.
- Ended task file with `TO BE VERIFIED` exactly as requested and did not begin execution.
- Important design note captured in plan: `ReleaseLeaderLease` requires adding a delete primitive to `DcsStore` (currently only write/drain exists), and dispatch should be best-effort with typed per-action errors.
- Next turn should perform DEEP skeptical verification (alter at least one plan item), then switch marker to `NOW EXECUTE` only when verified.

- Checked whether tests exercise real postgres and etcd3 in current repo state.
- Read harness and worker tests: postgres has real-process tests in pginfo::worker and process::worker, all gated by binary existence checks and early return if missing. etcd3 has harness spawn/shutdown test for real etcd binary, also gated by existence and early return.
- Ran targeted tests: spawn_etcd3_is_skipped_when_binary_missing, spawn_pg16_is_skipped_when_binaries_missing, real_start_and_stop_jobs_execute_binary_paths, step_once_transitions_unreachable_to_primary_and_tracks_wal_and_slots. All passed instantly in this environment because /usr/bin/etcd and /usr/lib/postgresql/16/bin/* are missing, so real-service paths were skipped.
- DCS logic tests currently use TestDcsStore/RecordingStore mocks; no test currently performs real etcd KV/watch operations end-to-end.
- Next if needed: install etcd + PG16 binaries at expected paths and rerun targeted real tests to validate actual runtime execution.

- User asked to remove skip-guard behavior so tests fail when postgres/etcd binaries are missing.
- Updated test guards in src/test_harness/etcd3.rs and src/test_harness/pg16.rs: renamed tests and replaced early return with assert! existence checks and clear missing-binary messages.
- Updated src/pginfo/worker.rs real postgres tests to assert required binaries exist (postgres/initdb and pg_basebackup for replica flow) instead of return.
- Updated src/process/worker.rs helper pg16_binaries from Option<BinaryPaths> to BinaryPaths with explicit panic listing missing binaries; removed all None=>return usage in real_* process tests.
- Ran cargo fmt.
- Validation blocked by existing unrelated compile failures in repo state: src/worker_contract_tests.rs uses non-existent HaWorkerCtx field `_private`; src/ha/mod.rs references missing module file src/ha/worker.rs.
- Next step if asked: fix unrelated compile blockers and rerun targeted tests to show hard failures on missing binaries in this environment.

- User requested centralizing binary path checks across tests with explicit instruction message.
- Added new shared helper module: src/test_harness/binaries.rs.
- Central helper now owns path constants and failure text: "couldn't find binary <path>, please either change path or install the binary for this to pass".
- Wired helper usage into src/test_harness/etcd3.rs, src/test_harness/pg16.rs, src/pginfo/worker.rs, and src/process/worker.rs (real PG job tests now use require_pg16_process_binaries via pg16_binaries wrapper).
- Added module export in src/test_harness/mod.rs.
- Ran cargo fmt and verified via ripgrep that only the centralized helper holds the binary-missing message and no old skip-guard patterns remain in the touched files.
- Full test execution still blocked by unrelated existing compile issues in worker_contract_tests and missing src/ha/worker.rs, so runtime proof of new panic text cannot be executed yet.

- User requested finding all soft acceptance of missing binaries and making them fail tests hard.
- Searched for skip guards (`None => return`, `exists() ... return`, and optional binary helper usage) and found all remaining cases in `src/test_harness/etcd3.rs`, `src/test_harness/pg16.rs`, `src/pginfo/worker.rs`, and `src/process/worker.rs` tests.
- Replaced optional/skip flows with hard-require helpers: switched all relevant tests to `require_etcd_bin`, `require_pg16_bin`, and `require_pg16_process_binaries`.
- Removed optional helper APIs from `src/test_harness/binaries.rs` (`optional_etcd_bin`, `optional_pg16_bin`, `optional_pg16_process_binaries`) to prevent future soft-skip reuse.
- Ran `cargo fmt`.
- Verified with ripgrep: no remaining `optional_*` binary helper usage and no `None => return` binary skip guards in the touched areas.
- Ran targeted tests to confirm hard failure behavior when binaries are missing in this env:
  - `cargo test test_harness::etcd3::tests::spawn_etcd3_requires_binary_and_spawns -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test test_harness::pg16::tests::spawn_pg16_requires_binaries_and_spawns -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test process::worker::tests::real_start_and_stop_jobs_execute_binary_paths -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test pginfo::worker::tests::step_once_transitions_unreachable_to_primary_and_tracks_wal_and_slots -- --nocapture`
- All targeted tests now fail immediately with the explicit missing-binary message from `src/test_harness/binaries.rs`.
- Note: I accidentally ran two cargo test commands in parallel once; observed artifact lock wait and then both still executed. Next time keep cargo gates strictly sequential to avoid lock contention noise.
- Next step if needed: install required binaries (`/usr/bin/etcd`, `/usr/lib/postgresql/16/bin/*`) and rerun the same tests to validate runtime behavior beyond the now-correct hard-missing-binary failure semantics.

- Policy update from PO: skipping tests due to missing binaries is strictly forbidden from now on.
- `optional` binary paths for PostgreSQL and etcd are explicitly forbidden in test flows.
- Any missing required binary (PG or etcd) must cause an immediate hard test failure.
- No soft acceptance, no early-return skip guards, and no optional fallback behavior are allowed for binary presence checks.

- User asked to install required test binary from GitHub release and then requested tooling scripts.
- Added repo-local tool installers:
  - tools/install-etcd.sh downloads etcd GitHub release (arch-aware) into .tools/etcd/bin/etcd.
  - tools/install-postgres16.sh downloads postgres source from GitHub tag (REL_16_11 by default), builds, and installs into .tools/postgres16/bin.
- Updated src/test_harness/binaries.rs constants to repo-local paths using CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR:
  - .tools/etcd/bin/etcd
  - .tools/postgres16/bin
- Updated .gitignore to ignore .tools/ so downloaded artifacts are not committed.
- Made both scripts executable and ran the etcd installer with escalated network access; etcd v3.6.8 successfully installed under .tools.
- Ran targeted etcd test; it now reaches harness execution but fails in this sandbox with "allocate ports failed: Operation not permitted", so full runtime validation is blocked by environment policy rather than missing binary.
- Next: if user wants, run tools/install-postgres16.sh on a machine with build deps and network, then rerun real binary tests outside the restricted sandbox.

- Continued per user direction and altered postgres installer away from GitHub-build flow to AlmaLinux dnf module flow.
- Rewrote tools/install-postgres16.sh to:
  - validate Linux + Alma/RHEL family,
  - run `sudo dnf module reset postgresql`, `sudo dnf module enable postgresql:16`,
  - run `sudo dnf install postgresql postgresql-server`,
  - symlink required binaries into `.tools/postgres16/bin` for repo-local paths used by tests.
- Executed updated postgres installer script successfully (module 16 enabled; packages already present; local symlink bin dir populated; postgres version 16.11 confirmed).
- Earlier etcd installer remained valid and had already installed etcd 3.6.8 into `.tools/etcd/bin/etcd`.
- Verified all expected binaries exist at repo-local paths (real etcd binary + postgres symlinks).
- Remaining limitation: full runtime harness tests still hit sandbox restrictions on local port allocation (`Operation not permitted`) in this environment.

- User asked current test status and requested add-bug tasks for failures.
- Ran gates: `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`, `make test-bdd` (captured logs under /tmp/test_status_make_*.log).
- Results observed:
  - make check: pass
  - make lint: pass
  - make test-bdd: pass
  - make test: failing/terminated with multiple failing tests and one long-running ports test warning.
- Reproduced representative failures with `--nocapture`; root cause is repeated `Operation not permitted (os error 1)` on port allocation in test harness paths:
  - src/test_harness/ports.rs
  - src/test_harness/etcd3.rs
  - src/pginfo/worker.rs
- Created grouped add-bug task per skill rules:
  - `.ralph/tasks/bugs/real-binary-tests-fail-when-port-allocation-is-blocked.md`
  - includes repro commands, observed failing tests, and standard acceptance criteria block.
